column5: I wanted to go for the top two spots as the payoff, even if a high decision number is made is higher than the £5.73 offered for the lower rankings. It was a tough decision regarding how much to sacrifice. I went for a number less than £5.75 to protect myself if I was unluckey on the random draw. But thankfully I recieved a good number to secure a top position even though I sacrificed £4.01. A great experiment though. Really enjoyed.

column7: Initially I thought to myself, if I selected 125 as my decision number- and my random number was 0- I would make a profit of 6.68 minimum. Then I considered doing 0- as my cost would be £0, and I would be guaranteed to make £5.73, so long as the random number still made me lowest. However I felt like this was the safest option, and I wanted to take a risk in hopes of making more. In hindsight, maybe I should have settled.
